docs.title: Peyton Pope
docs.text:
📄 My Resume

Articulate computer science student most interested in programming, digital media, sports, and above all— contributing to the Kingdom of God.

docs.title: Education
docs.text: Liberty University – Lynchburg, VA Expected Graduation: 2027 - B.S. Computer Science: Data Science & Software Engineering

docs.title: Experience
docs.text:
Software Development Intern – Pioneer Bible Translators Summer 2025 - Collaborated on an Elixir Phoenix web app for managing 500+ teammates and counselors - Participated in biweekly feedback meetings to refine features

Computer Science Tutor – Liberty University Dec 2024 – Present - Completed 100+ one-on-one appointments guiding students through programming concepts - Specialized in Data Structures & Algorithms, Advanced C++, and Object-Oriented-Programming

Resident Shepherd – Liberty University Feb 2024 – Present - Interviewed 30+ students and hired small group leaders; co-led a team of 10 peers to spiritually account for all hall members and foster a healthy hall culture through events and small groups

docs.title: College Football Playoff Predictor
docs.text: 🌐 Kaggle Notebook

docs.title: Objective
docs.text: A machine-learning based predictor for College Football Playoff outcomes built in a Kaggle notebook. The project uses historical college football data to model team performance and estimate playoff participation.

docs.title: Technology used
docs.text:
• Random Forest Classifier
  • XGBoost Classifier
  • Pandas, NumPy, Scikit-learn

docs.title: Skills Developed
docs.text:
• Data preprocessing and feature engineering from historical season data
  • Trains and evaluates a predictive model for CFP selection
  • Generates probabilities and comparative insights for playoff teams

View the Source Code

docs.title: Church Map: Data Science
docs.text:
🌐 View Project

A data science project mapping 200,000+ US churches by denomination, scraped and visualized to show geographical distributions across the United States.

docs.title: Objectives
docs.text:
• Collect and organize church data by scraping a large dataset (200k+ entries) with location coordinates.
  • Classify and filter churches by denomination using string matching and data cleaning.
  • Visualize geographical patterns of church presence using dot and contour plots on a US map.

docs.title: Tech Used
docs.text:
• Python with
    • BeautifulSoup (web scraping)
    • Pandas (data manipulation),
    • Matplotlib & Seaborn (visualization)

docs.title: Skills Developed
docs.text:
• Web scraping and automation
  • Large dataset cleaning and manipulation
  • Data visualization (dot maps, contour maps)

View the Source Code

docs.title: MyBlocker
docs.text: A simple Chrome extension to block distracting websites locally in your browser

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Block the current site in one click
  • Add and manage custom domains
  • Enable/disable sites with checkboxes

docs.title: Technology Used
docs.text:
• Vanilla JavaScript + Chrome Extension APIs.
  • Uses chrome.storage.local for persistent memory

docs.title: PMDb
docs.text:
🌐 Live Application

Interactive web app showcasing a Snowflake database of 37,000+ films with IMDb and Letterboxd ratings

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Explore film rankings across multiple rating systems with leaderboards filtering by decade, popularity, and rating

  • Compare IMDb vs Letterboxd lean using rating differentials to highlight where the platforms disagree most

docs.title: Data Pipeline Architecture
docs.text:
Layer Tool Description Ingestion Python, Snowflake Connector Python script loading raw Kaggle datasets into Snowflake database as bronze tables Modeling dbt Core Standardize types and filter nulls to create silver tables, Join both platforms on title + year then compute composite ratings and differentials into gold table Presentation Streamlit, Plotly, Snowflake SQLAlchemy Query Gold table live from Snowflake and render interactive charts and film lookup

View the Source Code

docs.title: SignCraft
docs.text:
🌐 Live Application

A resource for ASL learners to maintain vocabulary and practice grammar, with the intention of promoting Deaf community engagement

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Grow and maintain your ASL vocabulary with word bank for review and long-term retention.

  • Practice ASL-specific grammar patterns through contextual sentence building that reflects natural language use.

  • Promote engagement with the Deaf community and culture by learning vocabulary and expressions rooted in real-world, culturally relevant contexts.

docs.title: Technical used
docs.text:
• Backend Framework: Phoenix (Elixir/Erlang)
  • Database: PostgreSQL with Ecto
  • Frontend: Phoenix HEEX with Tailwind CSS
  • Deployment: Fly.io with Docker

docs.title: Skills developed
docs.text:
• Database relationships and migrations
  • Web app framework
  • RDMS deployment

View the Source Code
